title: Silicon Valley's next generation feels the AI blues
subhead: In California, the rise of artificial intelligence no longer generates the unanimous enthusiasm it once did. Anxiety is mounting, especially among students and engineers, as jobs have been cut and democratic risks have grown more threatening.

France's third heatwave of the year drags on, less intense than the previous one but still stifling
subhead: Twenty-four departments in northwestern France and the Paris region will be placed on red heat alert on Saturday, with 56 departments on orange alert. The very high temperatures could persist until Thursday.

title: Germany records most fatalities from Europe's latest heatwave
subhead: According to a report published on July 9 by the Robert Koch Institute, which oversees public health in Germany, deaths linked to high temperatures are estimated at more than 5,000. Experts argue that the country is significantly less prepared for heatwaves than France.

title: 'Foreign Tongues': The Rolling Stones ride a second wave of youth with new album
subhead: Without reinventing their formula, Mick Jagger and Keith Richards have delivered a catchy and revitalizing record that makes listeners forget the more uneven albums of the past few decades.

title: Le Monde's eight favorite exhibitions at Arles Photography Festival
subhead: Our culture critic has selected her favorite exhibitions among the 36 offerings at the major photography festival held throughout the southern French city.

subhead: Each time a new heatwave hits, the question of air conditioning and its environmental impacts crops up in the public debate. Here are the key points to help you understand what is at stake.

title: How Ukraine is turning Crimea, the Russians' Riviera, into a battlefield
subhead: The annexed Ukrainian peninsula, which Vladimir Putin sees as his crowning jewel, has descended into chaos as Kyiv ramps up attacks on the territory. Shocked tourists and residents express their distress as shortages worsen.

title: Eurostar sounds alarm over overcrowding at London's Saint Pancras station
subhead: As rail competition between Paris and London is set to arrive in the 2030s and with Eurostar having ordered new trains, limited station capacity – especially in London – is hampering market growth.
